topic: GPT-5 Turbo Review 2026: Native Image+Audio, Better JSON, April 7 Release

key_points: GPT-5.5 vs Claude comparison with benchmarks
40% fewer output tokens on Codex tasks
Natively omnimodal architecture (text, images, audio, video)
GPT-5.4 vs Claude Opus 4.6 vs Gemini 3.1 Pro comparison
Complete guide to OpenAI Structured Outputs in 2026
CFG engine enforces strict schema compliance at token level
JSON Mode legacy vs Strict Mode for production

stats.fact: GPT-5 was launched on August 7, 2025, making it the fifth in OpenAI's GPT series; mini and nano variants released alongside the flagship
GPT-5.4 mini (March 17, 2026) runs 2x+ faster than GPT-5 mini while approaching flagship GPT-5.4 performance on reasoning and coding benchmarks
stats.source: OpenAI API Docs - GPT-5.4 mini Model

stats.fact: GPT-5 achieves 94.6% on AIME 2025 math exam vs GPT-4o's 71%; SWE-bench Verified at 74.9% vs GPT-4o's 30.8%
GPT-5.4 achieves 75% on OSWorld computer use benchmark, surpassing the 72.4% human benchmark
GPT-5.5 (April 23, 2026) reduces hallucination rate by 60% compared to GPT-5.4; MRCR v2 at 1M tokens jumps from 36.6% to 74.0%
GPT-5.4 mini API pricing: significantly cheaper than flagship at approximately $0.15/$0.60 per million input/output tokens
GPT-5.5 uses 40% fewer output tokens on the same Codex tasks compared to GPT-5.4, reducing effective cost despite 2x price increase
GPT-5 structured outputs enforce strict JSON schema compliance at token generation level, eliminating malformed JSON that plagued GPT-4o
GPT-5 mini supports up to 400K context window with text and image inputs, significantly larger than GPT-4o mini's 128K
Gemini 3.1 Pro costs $2/$12 per million tokens vs GPT-5's $5/$30, delivering 60% cost saving with comparable reasoning on standard tasks

angles: GPT-5 Turbo (mini) as the successor to GPT-4o mini — why developers are upgrading now in 2026
Native image+audio in a single model: how GPT-5's unified multimodal architecture changes app development
Zero-hallucination JSON: GPT-5's structured output revolution and what it means for production APIs
Speed vs. intelligence tradeoff: when to use GPT-5 Turbo/mini vs. flagship GPT-5.5 for agentic workflows
Cost breakdown: GPT-5 Turbo vs Claude Haiku 4.5 vs Gemini 3.1 Flash — the budget AI model showdown in 2026
Real-world developer benchmarks: latency, token efficiency, and reliability across coding, RAG, and tool-use tasks
outline: H2: What Is GPT-5 Turbo? OpenAI's Fast, Multimodal Model Explained
H2: Key Features: Native Image & Audio Input Without Add-Ons
H2: Better JSON & Structured Outputs — Zero Schema Violations in Production
H2: Performance Benchmarks: GPT-5 Turbo vs. GPT-4o, Claude, and Gemini
H2: Pricing & Cost Analysis — When GPT-5 Turbo Beats the Competition
H2: Real Developer Use Cases: RAG, Agents, Vision APIs, Voice Apps
H2: GPT-5 Turbo API Quick Start: Image+Audio+JSON in One Call
H2: Limitations & When to Upgrade to GPT-5.5
H2: Verdict: Is GPT-5 Turbo the Best Fast Model in 2026?
research_notes: NOTE: OpenAI does not have an official product named 'GPT-5 Turbo' as of May 2026. The blog post title uses 'GPT-5 Turbo' analogously to 'GPT-4 Turbo' — the fast, efficient variant of GPT-5 (officially: GPT-5 mini / GPT-5.4 mini). The 'April 7 Release' in the task title may refer to an internal projected date or a minor API update. The closest actual releases are: GPT-5 mini (August 7, 2025), GPT-5.4 mini (March 17, 2026), and GPT-5.5 (April 23, 2026). Writer should frame the post around GPT-5 mini/GPT-5.4 mini as 'GPT-5 Turbo' and cover its key differentiators: native multimodal, improved structured outputs, speed/cost advantages.
